title: Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Nagaland

overview.short: Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Kohima District of Nagaland.
overview.description: Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Kohima District of Nagaland. It was first designated as a protected area in 1980. The park is named after the 2,296-metre (7,533 ft) mountain Pulie Badze, which means "Pulie's Seat", named after the legendary folktale character "Pulie". The park is home to a vast variety of birds like Tragopan Blythii (the state bird of Nagaland), White-naped Yuhina and Dark-rumped swift.

topPlaces.name: Mount Japfü
topPlaces.description: Mount Japfü (, YAP-FU) is a mountain peak of the Barail Range, located in Kohima District of Nagaland in India, about 15 kilometres (9 mi) south of Kohima, the capital of Nagaland.

topPlaces.name: Pulie Badze
topPlaces.description: Pulie Badze is a mountain peak in Kohima District of the Indian state of Nagaland, standing at an elevation of 2,296 m (7,533 ft) overlooking the capital city of Nagaland, Kohima. It is the centerpiece of Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Khonoma
topPlaces.description: Khonoma is a Western Angami Naga village located about 20 km west from Kohima, the capital of the Indian state of Nagaland. The village is referred to as Khwüno-ra (named after the Angami term for a local plant, Glouthera fragrantissima).

topPlaces.name: Kisama Heritage Village
topPlaces.description: Kisama Heritage Village (also Naga Heritage Village) is a Heritage Village located on the slopes of a hill between Kigwema and Phesama Villages in the Kohima District of the Indian state of Nagaland. The Heritage Village is the venue of the annual Hornbill Festival.

topPlaces.name: Sanctuary Falls
topPlaces.description: The Sanctuary Falls are a waterfall located in Khonoma in the Indian state of Nagaland. It lies within the Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Kohima Botanical Garden
topPlaces.description: Kohima Botanical Garden is a botanical garden in Kohima, Nagaland, India. It is located in the New Ministers' Hill Ward of Kohima.

topPlaces.name: New Ministers' Hill Ward
topPlaces.description: New Ministers' Hill Ward is a ward located under Nagaland's capital city, Kohima. The ward falls under the designated Ward No.

topPlaces.name: Lerie Ward
topPlaces.description: Lerie Ward is a ward located under Nagaland's capital city, Kohima. The ward falls under the designated Ward No.

items.note: Mount Japfü (, YAP-FU) is a mountain peak of the Barail Range, located in Kohima District of Nagaland in India, about 15 kilometres (9 mi) south of Kohima, the capital of Nagaland.

Pulie Badze is a mountain peak in Kohima District of the Indian state of Nagaland, standing at an elevation of 2,296 m (7,533 ft) overlooking the capital city of Nagaland, Kohima. It is the centerpiece of Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary.

Khonoma is a Western Angami Naga village located about 20 km west from Kohima, the capital of the Indian state of Nagaland. The village is referred to as Khwüno-ra (named after the Angami term for a local plant, Glouthera fragrantissima).

Kisama Heritage Village (also Naga Heritage Village) is a Heritage Village located on the slopes of a hill between Kigwema and Phesama Villages in the Kohima District of the Indian state of Nagaland. The Heritage Village is the venue of the annual Hornbill Festival.
The Sanctuary Falls are a waterfall located in Khonoma in the Indian state of Nagaland. It lies within the Khonoma Nature Conservation and Tragopan Sanctuary.
Kohima Botanical Garden is a botanical garden in Kohima, Nagaland, India. It is located in the New Ministers' Hill Ward of Kohima.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (42 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pulie Badze Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mount Japfü, Pulie Badze, Khonoma, Kisama Heritage Village, Sanctuary Falls.
